title: Minimal grounded-vs-blank-slate eval as an early go/no-go kill-gate for the optional explainer (handful of fixtures)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task builds a minimal grounded-vs-blank-slate eval over a handful of fixtures as an early go/no-go kill-gate for the optional explainer: if retrieval-grounded+cited does not at least trend ahead, the explainer is deferred and guides + data remain the product.
objective: Implement a minimal grounded-vs-blank-slate eval over a small fixture set that reports the delta as an early go/no-go for the explainer and asserts grounded answers emit zero invented eligibility numbers.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs benefits questions retrieval-grounded+cited vs. blank-slate on a small fixture set; reports the delta as an early go/no-go for the explainer
Asserts grounded answers emit zero invented eligibility numbers
If grounded+cited does not at least trend ahead, the explainer is deferred (guides + data remain the product) rather than forced
